Notice Of Motion And Motion For Assignment Order
Off Calendar Jun-27-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
PLAINTIFF TEACHERS INSURANCE AND ANNUITY ASSOC. OF AMERICA'S Motion For Assignment Order is GRANTED. No opposition filed. Moving party to prepare form of order that tracks the tentative ruling of the court. Judge: Richard Ulmer; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Robert Balian, CSR #5220 =302/hk

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Amend Judgment To Add Alter-Ego Judgment Debtor
Off Calendar Feb-28-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Eric Stephenson, Esq. 408-286-5100 of Pahl & McCay for Plaintiff TEACHERS INSURANCE AND ANNUITY ASSOC. OF AMERICA through Court Call.
No other Appearance.

RULING - The matter is Uncontested. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF TEACHERS INSURANCE AND ANNUITY ASSOC. OF AMERICA's Motion To Amend Judgment To Add Alter-Ego Judgment Debtor.

Plaintiff Teacher Insurance and Annuity Assoc. of America's motion to amend judgment to add alter ego judgment debtor is granted. No opposition filed.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Moving party to submit an amended judgment.

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (D302/HK)

Notice Of Prove-Up Hearing For Plaintiffs Request For Entry Of Default Judgment
Off Calendar Dec-06-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Dept. 514, Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: A. Mok. Proceedings reported by: Laura Martinez CSR# 11332.

Sarahann Shapiro, Esq., appearing for Plaintiff, Teachers Insurance and Annuity Association of America, a New York Corporation,
Order Granting Waiver of Witness Requirement of Hearing on Application for Default Prove-up, on file.
No appearance by defaulted defendant Lali International, Inc., a California Corporation,
Default of Defendant has been duly entered.

After Evidence and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiff and against Defaulted Defendant as follows:
Damages: $144,411.92
Pre-Judgment Interest: $0.00
Attorney's Fees: $5281.00
Costs: $630.00
Total Judgment in the amount of $150,322.92

Judgment to be reduced if Plaintiff secures a tenant for the space prior to expiration of the lease. Plaintiff will file a partial satisfaction of judgment for any rent actually received for the time frame covered by the subject lease.

Judgment signed in open court.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
